rule: Sources do not enter doctrine by presence. They enter only through classification, mapping, and conflict review.

tier_meaning.A: Primary/full-text sources that are currently usable for doctrine extraction with high trust.
tier_meaning.B: Usable sources with bounded trust: secondary fit, translation or OCR caveats, or technical references that must not control doctrine by themselves.
tier_meaning.C: Do not trust for doctrine extraction yet: summary/review material, extraction failure, or sources that require replacement or reprocessing first.

3: The Description Logic Handbook - Theory, Implementation and Applications (2003).json

sources.justification: Primary canonical source with usable extraction after noisy opening pages.
High-quality full text, but institutional-economics framing should support governance work rather than define the core layer.
Strong source quality, but doctrine fit is perspective-level and should not control canonical definitions.
Usable as a bounded secondary perspective source, not as a core doctrine source.
Primary canonical source with good extraction quality, but overlaps enough with Rawls that it can follow the initial rule/core pass.
Important canonical source, but OCR noise and older translation quality justify a second-pass position.
A book review/precis is not a doctrine source; use only as a pointer to stronger primary material.
Useful technical reference for structured knowledge modeling, but it must not drive broad architecture or ontology drift.
Operationally relevant, but the current extracted source is summary-style rather than a trusted primary/full-text edition.

sources.source: The Description Logic Handbook - Theory, Implementation and Applications (2003).json
sources.justification: High-value technical reference, but it should remain bounded as a technical aid rather than a doctrine driver.
Primary canonical source with strong structural value for concept discipline when kept within current bounded scope.
Canonical author priority is high, but the current JSON has no usable extracted text.
Secondary perspective source with usable extraction, but not part of the controlling doctrine layer.
Usable bounded perspective source; keep out of canonical definition control.
Best current source for falsifiability, refusal discipline, and failure clarity.
Useful logic support source, but not needed before the core integrity rules are stable.
Primary canonical source with high extraction quality and direct relevance to governance concepts.
Secondary perspective source with no usable extracted text in the current JSON.
Strong current-phase source for consistent rule application, edge-case handling, and bounded enforcement.
